---

## Approval Summary

KFR-REF-014A is approved as the Prototype A torso / jacket / pants garment detail reference.

This asset fills the previously identified gap for Kavo's under-harness clothing, pants cut, jacket shape, fabric details, and garment-to-harness compatibility.

Use the sheet as a visual production reference for:

- torso garment construction
- jacket length and collar logic
- under-layer shirt / field knit read
- harness clearance over torso layers
- pants cut, pockets, reinforcement panels, and cuffs
- boot compatibility
- tail clearance
- material breakup and paint zones

---

## Approved Garment Direction

### Jacket

Approved direction:

- cropped black cold-weather route jacket
- weathered leather / waxed shell read
- shearling-style collar translated as toy sculpt texture
- open-front layering option for harness visibility
- front zipper / seam logic
- lapel / seam treatment
- cuff treatment thick enough to print
- back yoke / seam shape visible but not over-detailed
- jacket length short enough to avoid blocking tail, hips, or harness lower straps

Use the jacket to communicate:

- cold-weather readiness
- boiler-ska underclass attitude
- practical route-worker toughness
- shelf-readable upper torso silhouette

### Under-Layer

Approved direction:

- black thermal / field-knit under-layer beneath the jacket and harness
- minimal graphic treatment unless separately approved
- keeps the torso from becoming bare monster anatomy or superhero armor
- supports harness readability

Do not treat any generated shirt graphic as canon. If a shirt mark is later desired, it must be reviewed separately.

### Pants

Approved direction:

- dark brown / worn brown route-worker cargo pants
- reinforced thigh and knee panels
- cargo pocket on one thigh
- smaller utility pocket on the other side if sculpt space allows
- wide belt loops and belt line
- tapered leg designed to tuck cleanly into or meet the field boot collar
- fabric folds simplified into bold toy-readable planes
- no fragile dangling straps or cords

Use the pants to communicate:

- route work
- field repair practicality
- durable movement
- Mountaineer utility
- stable toy stance

---

## Sculpt-Use Notes

- Keep fabric folds broad, not hyper-real miniature wrinkles.
- Sculpt cargo pocket flaps as sturdy raised panels, not thin free-floating pieces.
- Reinforced knee panels may help hide optional knee articulation if a later build includes it.
- Keep cuffs / hems thick enough to print and sand.
- Maintain boot collar clearance from KFR-REF-008.
- Maintain harness clearance from KFR-REF-010.
- Maintain simplified articulation clearance from KFR-REF-012.
- Maintain tail base and tail swing clearance from KFR-REF-009.
- Do not let jacket bulk interfere with shoulder movement.
- Do not let pants pockets interfere with hip movement.

---

## Paint-Use Notes

Use KFR-REF-014A with the approved Prototype A paint guide.

Suggested garment paint zones:

- jacket shell: worn black / charcoal weathered leather or waxed shell
- collar lining: warm gray / weathered shearling texture
- under-layer: black thermal / field knit
- pants base: worn brown / dark canvas brown
- reinforced thigh and knee panels: darker brown / black-brown
- belt: dark brown / black leather
- hardware: gunmetal or antique brass
- light weathering: W-02 Light Route Dust on lower pant legs, cuffs, knees, jacket hem, pocket edges, and boot-adjacent areas

Do not over-brighten the jacket or pants. The orange visibility language remains primarily on harness panels, boot uppers, and selected route-tool accents.

---

## Toy-Development Labels

### Canon Reference

Kavo remains a Velocisapien / Velociraptoroid Mountaineer, route guide, wilderness EMT, and GPR path-reader. The garment sheet must support that read without rewriting canon.

### Toy Adaptation Choice

The cropped cold-weather route jacket and dark route-worker cargo pants are approved as toy-specific garment solutions for Prototype A sculpt clarity, printability, paint breakup, and shelf presence.

### Prototype Engineering Choice

The jacket hem, pants cuffs, cargo pockets, knee panels, and collar thickness must be simplified for 5-inch print scale and joint clearance.

### Toy-Only Continuity / Candidate Canon

Specific shirt graphics, jacket patches, pocket symbols, garment brands, or unique badge meanings require separate review before any main-canon adoption.

---

## Guardrails

Do not use:

- real-world logos, brands, or exact garment trade dress
- generated text as final production copy
- loose long straps or dangling cords
- fragile buckles smaller than print-safe scale
- jacket bulk that blocks shoulder articulation
- pants bulk that blocks hip articulation
- bright fashion treatment that overwhelms Kavo's route gear
- military uniform read
- biker-gang parody read
- superhero jacket read
- ninja / assassin wardrobe cues
- child-safe or toy-certified language

---
